content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 ,J Street• Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322°5660 • Fax (916) 322•0886 
Dr. Charity Dean, MD, MPH 
Health Officer/ Staff Physician 
October 22, 2015 
Santa Barbara County Public Health Department 
345 Camino del Remedio 
Santa Barbara, CA 93110 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-15-193 
Dear Dr. Dean: 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding Government Code Section 1090 
and the financial disclosure provisions of the Political Refonn Act (the "Act").
1 Please note that we 
are only providing advice under the financial disclosure provisions of the Act and Section 1090, not 
under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest. We are 
also not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby ( 1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any 
advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
When a request for advice involves potential issues raised under Section 1090, the 
Commission is required to forward a copy of the request to the Attorney General's Office and the 
local district attorney prior to proceeding with the advice. (Section I 097.1 (c)(3).) Accordingly, we 
have forwarded your request to the Attorney General's Office and the Santa Barbara County 
District Attorney's Office. We did not receive a written response from the Attorney General's 
Office. The Santa Barbara County District Attorney's Office, however, responded and requested a 
copy of this letter. Finally, the following advice is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against 
any individual other than the requestor. (Section 1097.1 (c)(5).) 
QUESTIONS 
I. Does the Act require you to file statements of economic interests in your new position as 
Health Officer/ Staff Physician for the Santa Barbara County Public Health Department? 
2. IfCenCal Health offers you a payment for the outstanding balance of your student loans 
("student loan payoff') in exchange for a three-year work commitment and the fulfillment of related 
conditions, is that payment a "gift" or "income" under the Act? 
3. Would your acceptance of the student loan payoff be a violation of Section 1090? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


CONCLUSIONS 
File No. A-15-193 
Page No. 2 
I. Yes. You are required to file statements of economic interests at specific times and under 
certain circumstances because you are a "public official" under the Act. 
2. Because the potential student loan payoff would be in exchange for "consideration of 
equal or b'Teater value," it is "income" and not a "gift" under the Act, and if received it should be 
reported as income. 
3. No. Section I 090 does not prohibit you from accepting the student loan payoff because 
you have not made or participated in making a contract for purposes of Section I 090. 
FACTS 
You are the Health Officer/ Staff Physician of the Santa Barbara County Public Health 
Department (the "SBCPHD"). The Health Officer I Staff Physician investigates and reports disease 
and prevents and controls communicable disease, and is authorized to issue Health Officer Orders, 
to declare a health or local emergency, and to promote public health or safety through exercise of 
the police power. 
From January l, 2014 to June 21, 2015, you were temporarily assigned to be the Assistant 
Department Leader - Attorney I Physician, a designated employee position under the SBCPHD 
Conflict of Interest Code. As of June 22, 2015, you returned to a non-designated-employee position 
as a Staff Physician. On July 15, 2015, the Santa Barbara County Board of Supervisors approved 
your current role as Health Officer I Staff Physician of the SBCPHD. 
CenCal Health ("CenCal") is the regional health authority for Santa Barbara and San Luis 
Obispo counties, established by resolutions of those counties' boards of supervisors pursuant to the 
Santa Barbara San Luis Obispo Regional Health Authority Act (Chapter 3 (commencing with 
Section 101675) of Part 4 of Division 101 of the Health and Safety Code). CenCal administers 
Medi-Cal, a publicly funded health care program for low income individuals, for Santa Barbara and 
San Luis Obispo Counties. CenCal is a public entity governed by a 13-member Board of Directors 
appointed by the Santa Barbara and San Luis Obispo Counties' boards of supervisors. 
It is difficult to recruit physicians to the SBCPHD to serve the local Medi-Cal population 
and to retain them because the cost of living in or around Santa Barbara is very high and the salaries 
of SBCPHD physicians are much lower than those of comparable physicians working at local 
private clinics. For example, when you started at the SBCPHD in 2011, a comparable physician 
starting at a local private clinic earned 72 percent more than you; now, four years later, that 
comparable physician at the local private clinic earns 62 percent more than you. To recruit and 
retain physicians, CenCal offers hiring incentives for physicians that include student loan 
repayments, and has offered student loan repayments to physicians of other providers to encourage 
retention. CenCal determines the amount of each student loan payment ad hoc, based on the 
particular physician's outstanding student loan balance. 


File No. A-15-193 
Page No. 3 
You have an outstanding balance from your student loans. You have been advised that 
CenCal may offer you the student loan payoff in exchange for your commitment to remain in your 
current position for a period of three years, and during that time, to abide by SBCPHD policies and 
procedures and to achieve satisfactory reviews on annual performance evaluations. 
You have no authority over or influence on whether CenCal offers to make the student loan 
payoff. You have not attempted to influence any members of the CenCal Board of Directors in any 
way regarding the student loan payoff, or advocated for it in any way. The only people you have 
spoken with about the potential student loan payoff are your immediate supervisor SBCPHD 
Director Dr. Takashi Wada, MD, MPH and SBCPHD Medical Director Dr. Polly Baldwin, MD, 
MPH. 
ANALYSIS 
Statements of Economic Interests ("SEls") 
The Act's conflict of interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform their 
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the financial 
interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).) Toward that end, Section 87100 
prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her 
official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
In furtherance of this prohibition, the Act requires every state and local government agency 
to adopt a conflict of interest code. (Section 87300.) A conflict of interest code enumerates the 
positions within the agency that make or participate in making decisions that may have a 
foreseeable and material effect on any financial interest. (Section 87302(a).) A "designated 
employee" includes any "officer, employee, member, or consultant" of an agency whose position 
involves making or participating in making decisions which may have a foreseeable material effect 
on any financial interest. (Section 82019(a)(3).) Section 87302(a) requires the conflict of interest 
code to specify the economic interests a designated employee must report on his or her SEis. 
Section 87302(b) requires a designated employee to file SEls at times and under circumstances as 
specified. 
A conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes, participates in making, or uses 
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable 
material financial effect on one or more of his or her interests specified in Section 87103. A public 
official is subject to the Act's conflict of interest provisions and financial disclosure requirements. 
(Kendig Advice Letter, No. A-15-182; Haggerty Advice Letter, No. A-15-018; Haughy Advice 
Letter, No. I-t 3-122; Rosskopf Advice Letter, No. A-89-709.) 
Thus, to determine whether you must file SEls, we must first determine whether you are a 
"public official" who will be making, participating in making, or influencing a governmental 
decision. 


File No. A-15-193 
Page No. 4 
In making the determination of whether you are a "public official," the threshold inquiry is 
whether your employer, the SBCPHD, is a "local government agency" under the Act. Section 
82041 defines a "[l]ocal government agency" as "a county, city or district of any kind including 
school district, or any other local or regional political subdivision or any department, division, 
bureau, office, board, commission, or other agency of the foregoing." The SBCPHD is a 
"department" of the County of Santa Barbara and, therefore, a "local government agency" under the 
Act. 
Section 82048 defines "public official" as every member, officer, employee, or consultant of 
a state or local government agency. You are the Health Officer/ Staff Physician of the SBCPHD. 
Thus, you are an officer and an employee of a local government agency. Furthermore, as Health 
Officer I Staff Physician of the SBCPHD, you will regularly be making, participating in making, or 
influencing governmental decisions. The role of the Health Officer is to investigate and report 
disease and to prevent and control communicable disease, and the Health Officer is authorized to 
issue Health Officer Orders, to declare a health or local emergency, and to promote public health or 
safety through exercise of the police power. Because you are an officer and employee of a local 
government agency, you are a "public official" under the Act. 
Accordingly, we conclude that the Act requires you to file SEls at specified times and under 
certain circumstances. We suggest you review with the Santa Barbara County filing officer to 
determine your disclosure category and the economic interests you are required to report. 
"Gift" or "Income" 
We now address the second question presented, whether receipt of the student loan payoff 
would qualify as a "gift" or "income" under the Act. Section 82028 defines a "gift" as: 
"(a) ... any payment that confers a personal benefit on the recipient, to the 
extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received and includes 
a rebate or discount in the price of anything of value unless the rebate or 
discount is made in the regular course of business to members of the public 
without regard to official status." (Emphasis added.) 
And Section 82030 defines "income" as: 
"(a) ... a payment received, including but not limited to any salary, wage, 
advance, dividend, interest, rent proceeds from any sale, gift, including any 
gift of food or beverage, loan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness received 
by the filer, reimbursement for expenses, per diem, or contribution to an 
insurance or pension program paid by any person other than an employer, and 
including any community property interest in the income of a spouse." 
(Emphasis added.) 
Therefore, whether the student loan payoff is a "gift" or "income" under the Act depends on 
whether "consideration of equal or greater value" is received in return for the payment. 


File No. A-15-193 
Page No. 5 
Based on the facts provided, it is difficult to recruit physicians to the SBCPHD to serve the 
Medi-Cal population because the cost of living in or around Santa Barbara is very high and the 
salaries of SBCPHD physicians are much lower than those of comparable physicians working at 
local private clinics. To recruit and retain physicians, CenCal offers hiring incentives for physicians 
that include student loan repayments, and has offered student loan repayments to physicians of 
other providers to encourage retention. 
In this instance, the student loan payoff CenCal may offer you is in exchange for you 
committing to remain in your current position for a period of three years, and during that time, to 
abide by SBCPHD policies and procedures and to achieve satisfactory reviews on annual 
performance evaluations. In light of the need for physicians in the area, and the need for incentives 
such as student loan repayments to recruit and retain physicians, we find that the potential student 
loan payoff would be "consideration of equal or greater value." Thus, we conclude that the potential 
student loan payoff would be "income," and not a "gift," under the Act, and if you receive it, it 
should be reported as income on your SEI.2 
Section I 090 
We employ the following analysis to determine whether an official has a conflict of interest 
under Section I 090. 
Arc you subject to the provisions of Section 1090? 
Section 1090 applies to virtually all state and local officers, employees, and multi-member 
bodies, whether elected or appointed. You are the Health Officer I Staff Physician for the SBCPHD. 
As such, you are an officer and an employee of a local government agency, and therefore, you are 
subject to the provisions of Section 1090. 
Docs the decision at issue involve a contract? 
To determine whether a decision involves a contract, one may look to general principles of 
contract law (84 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 34, 36 (2001 ); 78 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 230, 234 (1995)), while 
keeping in mind that "specific rules applicable to Sections 1090 and I 097 require that we view the 
transactions in a broad manner and avoid narrow and technical definitions of 'contract"' (People, .. 
Honig, supra, at p. 351 citing Stigall, supra, at pp. 569, 571.) 
The decision at issue here is whether CenCal should extend the offer for the student loan 
payoff to you in exchange for your commitment to remain in your current position for a period of 
three years, and during that time, to abide by SBCPHD policies and procedures and to achieve 
satisfactory reviews on annual performance evaluation. The decision involves a contract. 
2 You have also asked whether CenCal may directly pay the student loan payoff to your student loan lender. 
Neither the Act nor Section I 090 would preclude CenCal from directly paying the student loan lender the student loan 
payoff on your behalf, but the reporting requirement would still apply. 


Arc you making or participating in making a contract? 
File No. A-15-193 
Page No. 6 
It is important to note that Section I 090 reaches beyond the officials who actually execute 
the contract. Section l 090 casts a wide net to capture those officials who participate in any way in 
the making of the contract. (People v. Sobel (1974) 40 Cal.App.3d 1046, 1052.) Therefore, for 
purposes of Section 1090, participation in the making of a contract is defined broadly as any act 
involving preliminary discussions, negotiations, compromises, reasoning, planning, drawing of 
plans and specifications, and solicitations for bids. (Millbrae Assn. for Residential Sun•ival 1•. City 
of Millbrae ( 1968) 262 Cal.App.2d 222, 23 7; see also Stigall v. City of Taft ( 1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 
569.) Individuals in advisory positions can influence the development of a contract during these 
early stages of the contracting process even though they have no actual power to execute the final 
contract. (See, e.g., Schaefer, supra, 140 Cal.App.2d at p. 291; City Council v. McKinley ( 1978) 80 
Cal.App.3d 204.) 
Based on the facts presented, you have no authority over or influence on whether CenCal 
offers to make the student loan payoff. You have not attempted to influence any members of the 
CenCal Board of Directors in any way regarding the student loan payoff, or advocated for it in any 
way. The only people you have spoken with about the potential student loan payoff are your 
immediate supervisor SBCPHD Director Dr. Takashi Wada, MD, MPH and SBCPHD Medical 
Director Dr. Polly Baldwin, MD, MPH. The amount of each student loan payment made by CenCal 
to recruit or retain a physician is determined ad hoc, based on the physician's outstanding student 
loan balance, and therefore, there is no negotiation of the amount of the payment. 
Accordingly, we find that you have not made or participated in making a contract for 
purposes of Section 1090. Thus, our analysis under Section I 090 ends here, and we conclude that 
Section 1090 does not prohibit you from accepting the student loan payoff. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
MFC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
* 
By: 
Matthew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division

3. No. Section I 090 does not prohibit you from accepting the student loan payoff because 
you have not made or participated in making a contract for purposes of Section I 090.

Statements of Economic Interests ("SEls")

2. Because the potential student loan payoff would be in exchange for "consideration of

Finally, the following advice is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any individual other than the requestor. (Section 1097.1 (c)(5).) QUESTIONS I. Does the Act require you to file statements of economic interests in your new position as Health Officer/ Staff Physician for the Santa Barbara County Public Health Department? 2. IfCenCal Health offers you a payment for the outstanding balance of your student loans ("student loan payoff') in exchange for a three-year work commitment and the fulfillment of related conditions, is that payment a "gift" or "income" under the Act? 3.
